title: schoenolirion bracteosum
intro: Schoenolirion bracteosum, also known as the bracted bog-gentian or marsh gentian, is a rare and short-lived perennial plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is often found growing in wet, grassy areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The roots of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are believed to help alleviate respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma, and promote overall well-being.
